codeReview.summary: The feature successfully opened the intended guest browse routes and preserved login callback behavior on detail-page CTAs, but it did not fully meet the trust-foundation contract. Trust messaging landed on home, detail, and some account surfaces, yet no category listing page received visible trust architecture, so listing surfaces called out in the feature description are still missing the required trust modules.

issues.description: No Buyer Protection module, trust badges, or refund/support/guarantee messaging was added to the football category listing page. The feature description explicitly requires visible trust architecture on listing surfaces, and the same omission also applies to the basketball and concert category listing pages.

The basketball listing page remains unchanged for trust architecture. Guests can browse it, but there are no trust modules or trust copy near the browsing/decision surface despite the feature requiring listing-surface trust coverage.

The concert listing page also lacks any of the requested trust architecture elements. This leaves one of the mandated public listing surfaces without Buyer Protection, badge, or support/refund messaging coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The marketplace-worker skill requires agent-browser verification for guest browsing and gated transaction flows, but the reviewed worker only recorded curl-based checks and no interactive browser evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/wendy/wcstubhub-clone/.factory/skills/marketplace-worker/SKILL.md step 5 requires agent-browser; handoff /Users/wendy/.factory/missions/bc99579a-c2db-4a67-aad4-2d259a3c559d/handoffs/2026-03-26T06-05-07-020Z__guest-browsing-and-trust-foundation__924e7289-aa44-4908-9f93-7500b1074f23.json has verification.interactiveChecks = [] and only curl commands for route validation.

summary: Scrutiny failed: guest browsing and CTA callback redirects were implemented, but the required trust architecture was not added to the football, basketball, or concert listing pages, so listing-surface coverage is incomplete. I also noted that the worker skipped the skill-required agent-browser verification and relied only on curl checks in the handoff.
